title: Comparing Your Real Options for Selling Software Without a Stripe Account — No PayPal Required
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal and Stripe block creators in dozens of countries. Here is the infrastructure that works everywhere they don't.

content.text: You’ve built your software. It’s ready to sell. But here’s the wall: Stripe and PayPal block Nigeria. Gumroad and Payhip require them. You’re stuck. Crypto is your only real option—but which tools actually work? This isn’t about hype. It’s about getting paid. Let’s compare your real options for selling software without Stripe or PayPal. No fluff. No false promises. Just what works.
What Each Option Actually Offers

Stripe and PayPal offer payment processing, but they’re off-limits in Nigeria. Gumroad and Payhip are built on them, so they’re out too. Crypto-native tools like Unchained Commerce’s Web3 Wallet Connect ($19.99) let you accept 300+ coins directly into your wallet. No middlemen. No accounts. Just QR codes and live price feeds. It’s not as polished as Stripe, but it’s global and permissionless. Other options? Local payment gateways like Paystack, but they’re limited to NGN and often require KYC. Crypto wins for accessibility, but you’ll need to educate buyers on how to pay with USDT or BTC. Tradeoff: simplicity for freedom.
How They Handle Restricted Countries

Stripe and PayPal exclude Nigeria due to regulatory and fraud risks. Gumroad and Payhip inherit these restrictions. Local gateways like Paystack work within Nigeria but fail for international buyers. Crypto-native tools like Web3 Wallet Connect bypass these blocks entirely. They don’t care about your location. If you can access the internet, you can sell. The catch? Volatility and buyer education. But if you’re already blocked, this is your only global option. It’s not perfect, but it’s permanent.
Payment Speed, Fees, and Real Costs

Stripe and PayPal take 2-7 days to settle funds. Crypto? Instant. But network fees vary. A $10 sale in USDT costs ~$0.50 in fees on Tron, $1.50 on Ethereum. Web3 Wallet Connect supports multi-chain payments, so you can choose the cheapest option. Local gateways charge 1.5-3.5% per transaction plus NGN conversion fees. Crypto’s fees are higher for small transactions but cheaper for larger ones. Real cost? Time spent educating buyers vs. waiting weeks for payouts. Crypto wins for speed and control.
PayPal's geographic exclusion is not a bug — it is a deliberate infrastructure decision that will not change.
Automation and Delivery Capabilities

Stripe and PayPal integrate with platforms like Gumroad for automated delivery. Crypto tools like Unchained Commerce’s Core Shop Engine ($49.99) automate delivery too—no payment processor account required. Web3 Wallet Connect handles the frontend, but you’ll need a backend for automation. Local gateways? Manual delivery unless you build custom integrations. Crypto’s automation is less plug-and-play but more flexible. If you’re tech-savvy, it’s a better long-term solution.
Which One to Choose Based on Your Situation

If you’re selling to Nigerians only, Paystack works—but it’s limited. For global sales, crypto is your only option. Web3 Wallet Connect is cheap ($19.99) and covers 300+ coins. Pair it with Core Shop Engine for full automation. If you’re selling high-value software, crypto’s fees are negligible. If you’re selling $5 presets, local gateways might be cheaper—but you’ll lose international buyers. Crypto isn’t easy, but it’s the only way to sell globally without Stripe or PayPal. Choose based on your audience, not your comfort zone.
How do I sell digital products if PayPal is not available in my country?

If you can’t use Stripe or PayPal in Nigeria, crypto-native tools like Web3 Wallet Connect are your best option. They accept 300+ coins, require no KYC, and work globally. Pair it with a backend like Core Shop Engine for automated delivery. Local gateways like Paystack work for NGN sales but fail internationally. Crypto has higher fees for small transactions but offers instant payouts and no geographic restrictions. It’s not simple, but it’s the only way to sell globally without traditional payment processors.
Crypto-native infrastructure means no single platform decision can cut off your income.
Related: How Creators Like You Are Getting Paid

If you’re selling ebooks, courses, or templates, the same rules apply. Crypto-native infrastructure like Web3 Wallet Connect works for any digital product. The key is educating buyers on how to pay with USDT or BTC. For a full setup, combine it with Core Shop Engine for automated delivery and Admin Dashboard for order management. It’s more work upfront, but it’s the only way to sell globally without Stripe or PayPal.
Your Action Checklist

1. Choose a crypto payment tool like Web3 Wallet Connect ($19.99) for frontend integration. 2. Set up a backend like Core Shop Engine ($49.99) for automated delivery. 3. Educate buyers on paying with USDT or BTC—include a step-by-step guide in your store. 4. Test the flow with a small transaction to ensure delivery works. 5. Launch with a clear refund policy to build trust. 6. Promote to your audience and track sales in the Admin Dashboard ($29.99).
The store that runs on a wallet and a webhook cannot be suspended by anyone else's compliance team.
Web3 Wallet Connect — $19.99. Frontend wallet integration with multi-chain QR generation and live price feeds. Let buyers pay in BTC, ETH, USDT, SOL, and 300 more coins.
